title: Seattle Crime, 1985–2026 — Four Decades Mapped, Neighborhood by Neighborhood
description:
Four decades of reported crime in Seattle, Washington — from the 1987 peak to today, mapped neighborhood by neighborhood.

This is a fully sourced, reproducible data visualization. Every figure comes straight from the FBI Uniform Crime Reporting program (1985–2016) and the Seattle Police Department's public-domain open data (2017–2026, by MCPP neighborhood). Every dot on the map is a real reported offense location, anonymized to the block by SPD; redacted locations are counted but never plotted. Coverage and caveats are shown on screen.

▶ CHAPTERS
0:00  Hook — the fall from the 1987 peak
0:08  What you're about to see
0:22  Chapter 1 — the long arc, 1985–2025
2:30  The last five years — up close
2:43  Chapter 2 — 58 neighborhoods, month by month
4:52  The busiest & safest neighborhoods
5:18  Sources & credits

▶ DATA SOURCES
• Seattle Police Department Crime Data (2008–present, Public Domain), data.seattle.gov
• FBI Uniform Crime Reporting / Crime Data Explorer (1985–2016), ORI WASPD0000
• Seattle MCPP (Micro-Community Policing Plan) neighborhood boundaries, Seattle CityGIS

▶ MADE BY AN AI — FULLY TRANSPARENT
This video was produced end-to-end by Claude (Anthropic's AI): it located and fetched the official source data, built and validated the data pipeline, verified every on-screen figure against the sources, designed and rendered the visuals, and generated the music. No human edited the numbers. Every step — code, data, and design decisions — is public and reproducible in the repository linked below. If you find an error, open an issue; corrections are part of the record.

▶ CODE, DATA & FULL SOURCES (reproduce every number)
https://github.com/hadi-nayebi/crime-cartography/tree/main/videos/seattle-wa

Music generated with Stable Audio Open (Stability AI).

If this was worth a look, please Like, Subscribe, and Share it with someone from Seattle.

#Seattle #CrimeData #DataVisualization #OpenData
